title: task
description: commands.rs:186-188 - On non-Unix platforms, format!("{seed_hex}\n") creates a temporary String containing the private key seed that is never zeroized. The seed hex copy remains in memory after the function returns, potentially recoverable by an attacker with memory access.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced std::fs::write(format!(...)) with File::create + writeln! to avoid creating a temporary String that would leak seed material in memory. The writeln! macro writes directly to the file handle without intermediate allocation.
